identity.one-liner: Roguelike dungeon crawl where you negotiate as much as you fight.
identity.reference: Pokemon is a reference for encounter system, types, and sprites.
identity.pacing: Target ~1 dungeon floor cleared per game hour. Main driver: loot.

player-loop.success-criteria: In 5 minutes: "I cared about one choice." Avoid: unclear what to do next.

navigation.model: Graph of rooms. Each room has: id, name, description, type, exits (direction → target room id). No tile-based movement — player chooses exits from buttons/choices.
navigation.floor-map: Show current floor as a simple list or grid of rooms. Player sees: current room highlighted, available exits as buttons, room types (combat, dialogue, treasure, rest, boss).
navigation.fog: Rooms start undiscovered. Adjacent rooms become visible when you enter a neighboring room. Boss room is always visible.
navigation.simplified-navigation: For the vertical slice, navigation can be simplified to button choices: "Go North (Combat Room)", "Go East (Treasure Room)". A full graphical map is NOT required — functional room-to-room movement is.

dungeon.floors: Multiple dungeon floors. Each floor has rooms connected by exits. Boss guards the exit to the next floor.
dungeon.tick-system: Dungeon tick advances when player enters a room. Boss spawns enemies every 3 ticks from adjacent rooms.

combat.flow: Turn-based. Player acts, then enemy acts. Repeat until one side is defeated or player flees.

actions.action: Fight — basic attack using Might stat vs enemy Defense
Spells — cast a prepared spell (costs mana)

stats.stat: Mana — spell resource, spent on casting

combat.rewards: Win = XP + mana crystals. Lose = game over (or retreat to last rest room).
combat.ui: Show: enemy sprite/name, enemy HP bar, player HP/mana bars, action buttons. Keep it simple and visible.

dialogue.flow: Player enters dialogue room, sees NPC portrait and greeting text. Player chooses from dialogue options. Some options may be gated by stats or items.
dialogue.outcomes: Dialogue can give: items, quest info, lore, stat changes, shop access.
dialogue.ui: Show: NPC sprite/portrait, dialogue text, choice buttons. Simple and readable.

layers.layer: entityType — physical kind (human, goblin, dragon, etc.)
archetype — build/stats derived from title (wanderer, warrior, mage, etc.)

entities.content-driven: All entity data lives in JSON content packs. No hardcoded entities.
entities.sprites: Use placeholder sprites. Pokemon-style pixel art or simple colored shapes are fine for the eval.

spells.model: Spells are composed of runes. 26 runes (A-Z). A spell = ordered list of rune IDs.
spells.slots: Player has limited spell slots (prepared spells). Can swap at forge rooms.
spells.casting: In combat: choose Spells action, pick a spell, spend mana, apply effect.
spells.forge: At forge rooms: combine runes to create new spells. Simple UI: pick runes, name spell, confirm.
spells.for-vertical-slice: Start with 3-5 pre-made spells the player gets. Forge can be phase 2. Focus on: spells work in combat, mana is spent, effects are visible.

economy.currency: Mana crystals. Earned from combat wins and treasure chests.
economy.shops: NPCs in dialogue rooms may sell items for crystals.
economy.golden-crystal: Boss drops golden crystal (high value) — or offers a deal to let you pass.

ui.gameplay-ui: The main game view during dungeon exploration. Shows: room info, navigation choices, HUD (HP/mana/floor/tick). This is where the player spends most of their time.
ui.display-menus: Overlay menus that open from a header/toolbar: Map, Bag, Spellbook, Stats, Settings. These overlay the game view, they don't replace it.
ui.hud: Always visible during gameplay: player name, HP bar, mana bar, current floor, dungeon tick count.
ui.principle: Clarity over polish. The player must always know: where am I, what can I do, what are my stats. Fancy graphics are NOT needed — clear text and functional buttons are enough.

persistence.method: localStorage. Save full game state (player stats, inventory, position, floor progress, discovered rooms).
persistence.auto-save: Auto-save on room entry. Manual save from Settings menu.

content-packs.principle: All game data is in JSON files loaded at startup. No hardcoded game rules in code.

packs.pack: floors.json — room layouts per floor, exits, room types
spells.json — pre-made spells with rune compositions

build.stack: Vite + TypeScript + KAPLAY (web game framework). Single page app.
build.no-tile-movement: No character walking on grids. Navigate by choosing exits.
build.vertical-slice-priority: Build a playable vertical slice first. 3 rooms, working combat, visible UI. Then add depth. A blank screen scores 0.
